text: Staged collision: claimant arranges or stages a collision between vehicles, often with a confederate driver. Indicators: passenger count exceeds available seat-belts, all occupants treat at same chiropractic clinic, claimant has prior staged claims, vehicles low value, low speed but high reported injury, witnesses unrelated to claimant unavailable.

Phantom damage: claimant reports damage that pre-existed the alleged loss. Indicators: damage pattern inconsistent with reported impact, paint/dirt suggests pre-existing wear, prior repair invoices match same damage, photos pre-date claim per EXIF metadata.

Arson-for-profit: insured deliberately damages property post-coverage-increase to claim policy proceeds. Indicators: coverage increased shortly before loss, financial distress preceded loss, insured moved or removed valuables shortly before fire, multiple ignition points, accelerant detected, claimant alibi for time of fire.

Falsified burglary: insured fabricates a break-in to claim contents loss. Indicators: no signs of forced entry but interior staged, items reported stolen never owned (no receipts, no photos), high-value items only stolen, neighborhood crime rate inconsistent.

Malingering / exaggerated injury: claimant exaggerates injury or fakes ongoing disability. Indicators: subjective complaints with no objective findings, claimant observed performing activities inconsistent with claimed disability (social media, surveillance), claimant misses scheduled IMEs, multiple prior similar claims.

Provider upcoding: medical provider bills for more complex / expensive procedures than performed. Indicators: ICD/CPT codes inconsistent with diagnosis, provider's coding skew is much higher than peer providers, repeated unbundling of related procedures, services billed during patient's known travel/hospitalization dates.

Ghost broker: third party sets up policies on behalf of claimants who do not know they are insured, then claims against them. Indicators: policyholder unaware of policy, premium paid from third-party account, vehicle reported stolen shortly after policy issuance, claim filed by intermediary not by policyholder.

Soft fraud: legitimate claim exaggerated for higher payout. Indicators: claimed loss inventory inflated beyond market value, claimed contents includes items typical to fabricate (cash, jewelry, untracked electronics), repair estimate inflated above market by 30%+.

Multiple-line red flag: claimant filed losses across multiple insurance lines in short time (auto + home + life). Indicators: 3+ claims across lines within 24 months, no obvious life-event reason (move, marriage, birth, etc.).
